events.title: Which company has the best AI model end of May?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 83.5% implied probability to Anthropic holding the top spot on the Chatbot Arena Text leaderboard by May 31, propelled by its Claude Opus 4.7-thinking model's commanding 1503 Elo score—well ahead of Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview at 1492 and OpenAI's GPT-5.5-high at eighth place. Anthropic's April 16 release of Opus 4.7 delivered superior performance in crowdsourced blind evaluations across reasoning, coding, and prose generation, as validated by recent leaderboard updates through early May. Google garners 14.5% on anticipation of Gemini 4.0 previews at I/O (May 19-20), while OpenAI's 2.5% reflects GPT-5.5's underwhelming benchmark gains amid fierce competition; lower-odds challengers like xAI lag due to internal setbacks. With three weeks to resolution, surprise model drops could shift dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors Anthropic with a 68.5% implied probability for the best AI model by June 30, 2026, driven by Claude Opus 4.7's recent dominance across Arena.ai leaderboards, including #1 rankings in Code Arena for agentic coding tasks like building live websites, Document Arena for long-context reasoning, and top spots in Domain categories such as Gaming, Simulations, and Brand & Marketing as of early May. Frequent Anthropic releases, like Opus 4.7 in April, have widened its lead over competitors, with massive Elo gaps (e.g., +37 points in Code Arena). Google trails at 21.5% amid Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview's solid but secondary showings and I/O 2026 previews, while OpenAI's GPT-5.5 (launched April) holds 6.5% despite strong Intelligence Index scores, reflecting uncertainty in upcoming benchmarks. Key watch: potential Gemini advancements at Google I/O and final leaderboard shifts before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 81.5% implied probability to Anthropic holding the #1 spot on the Arena.ai Chatbot Arena leaderboard (Style Control On) by May 31, propelled by Claude Opus 4.7's commanding leads in recent domain-specific evaluations released May 8—topping categories like coding, gaming, simulations, and brand design with margins up to 42 Elo points over rivals. The model's agentic coding prowess, demonstrated in Code Arena since mid-April, has solidified its frontier position amid stagnant updates from OpenAI's GPT-5.x and Google's Gemini 3.1. Google's 12.5% share reflects speculation on a Gemini refresh, but no confirmed timelines exist before expiry, underscoring Anthropic's skin-in-the-game edge while new releases remain a wildcard.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Anthropic at 52% implied probability for the best math AI model by May's end, driven by Claude Opus 4.7's April 16 release, which delivered benchmark gains in sustained reasoning on challenging tests like FrontierMath Tier-4, surpassing prior versions and narrowing gaps with rivals despite OpenAI's dominance in raw MATH-500 (99.4%) and AIME scores via GPT-5.5 (April 23 launch). OpenAI holds 35.5% as GPT-5 variants lead many leaderboards, but traders anticipate potential slippage without new drops. Google's 14.5% reflects May 8's AI co-mathematician multi-agent system hitting 48% on FrontierMath autonomously, though single-model parity lags. Google I/O looms as a key catalyst amid tight AI math race.

events.title: Trump orders federal review of AI model releases by May 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump signs any federal legislation into law or performs any executive action that creates, authorizes, or directs a federal government review process for the public release of new artificial intelligence models by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

A qualifying action must create a federal process for reviewing or approving the public release of new artificial intelligence models. A qualifying review process may apply to artificial intelligence models generally, only to models meeting specified criteria (e.g.capability, safety, cybersecurity, national-security, or other risk-based criteria), or to models selected for review at the discretion of the federal government.

Legislation or executive actions which create a group or committee responsible for overseeing artificial intelligence matters will only qualify if they explicitly create a qualifying review process.

Non-binding statements, proposals, unconfirmed reports, or federal review of artificial intelligence models solely for government procurement or internal government use will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the United States federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

A qualifying review process may apply to artificial intelligence models generally, only to models meeting specified criteria (e.g.capability, safety, cybersecurity, national-security, or other risk-based criteria), or to models selected for review at the discretion of the federal government.

Legislation or executive actions which create a group or committee responsible for overseeing artificial intelligence matters will only qualify if they explicitly create a qualifying review process.

Non-binding statements, proposals, unconfirmed reports, or federal review of artificial intelligence models solely for government procurement or internal government use will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the United States federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
